source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Emergency Disconnect Sequence Activation

actions_taken: ROV inspection of LMRP and BOP stack
ROV inspection of sea floor to locate BOP stack
event.summary: On September 11, 2016, at 0826 hours, the Senior Dynamic Positioning Officer (SDPO) aboard the Diamond Ocean Blackhornet mistakenly activated the Emergency Disconnect Sequence (EDS) while the Blow Out Preventer (BOP) stack was suspended approximately 1,550 feet below the water line during a planned pull to surface. The EDS activation caused the BOP stack to fall approximately 2,330 feet to the sea floor. The incident resulted from human error triggered by confusion between similar acronyms (EDS vs ESD), similar personnel names, lack of pre-job planning, and informal EDS testing practices. The BOP stack landed safely in the designated safe zone approximately 484 feet from the wellhead with no damage to the rig, no personnel injury, and no environmental impact.
recommendations: Include Bridge personnel (DPOs) in pre-job, face-to-face notifications before tests are conducted
Require a Subsea Engineer to be present at each EDS station during tests
Place a sign over EDS buttons when the BOP is unlatched warning that these buttons are not to be touched while unlatched
Require the Offshore Installation Manager (OIM), Rig Superintendent, or Tool Pusher to be notified prior to EDS tests
Consider placing an inhibit switch on the EDS that would only be used when the BOP is unlatched
Revise ESD test procedures so that Onward no longer supervises the weekly ESD test; responsibility transferred to Anadarko Lead Drilling Supervisor
Station Anadarko employees and the rig's Tool Pusher at the three ESD stations during ESD tests
Do not conduct ESD tests while the BOP stack is unlatched

hazards.name: Subsea equipment handling and BOP operations

threats.name: Unintended EDS activation during BOP pull operations
Confusion between EDS and ESD test requests
Lack of situational awareness regarding BOP status

consequences.name: BOP stack drop to sea floor
Potential personnel injury from sudden rig movement

controls.name: EDS dual-button activation requirement
controls.lod_basis: Design feature requiring simultaneous push of two buttons

supporting_text: The EDS is manually activated, and it is initiated via two buttons that must be pushed at the same time in order to fire the EDS. One is a 'push to operate' and the other is the button to actually fire the system.
The SDPO turned to the EDS panel, and activated the EDS sequence.

controls.name: Multiple EDS stations at manned locations

supporting_text: There are multiple EDS stations located in various manned locations on the rig, such as the Bridge, Rig Floor at each drilling chair, and each BOP station.
The EDS panel was noted as being only a few feet from where the SDPO was sitting at the time the phone was answered

controls.name: Pre-job planning and briefing for EDS/ESD tests
controls.barrier_role: Ensure personnel awareness and clarity before test execution

supporting_text: Lack of pre-job planning. The Bridge personnel (DPOs') were not involved with pre-job planning before ESD or EDS tests were conducted. They were not notified until they were called and asked to make a function.

During these tests the DPOs would receive a simple phone call with the request for them to push the EDS buttons. There was no pre-job planning or discussion that otherwise would have given them time to think about the test being conducted.

controls.name: Subsea Engineer oversight of EDS testing
controls.barrier_role: Provide technical supervision and verification of EDS tests

supporting_text: Informality of EDS testing in the past. Subsea Engineers normally oversee BOP functions but were not present when several EDS functions were made previous to the incident.
On several occasions during the software upgrades and during the stump test, the Dynamic Positioning Officers (DPOs) were asked to initiate the EDS to test the system.

controls.name: Distinct panel design and labeling for EDS vs ESD
controls.barrier_role: Prevent confusion between similar emergency systems
controls.lod_basis: Design differentiation to reduce human error

supporting_text: The panels and buttons also look nothing alike. The EDS panel requires the 'push to operate' button to be pushed as well as the EDS activation button. The ESD station is a pull type, where the button is pulled out rather than pushed in to activate its function.
Though the acronyms are similar because the same letters are used in different order, the panels and functions are very different.

controls.name: Immediate recognition and response to EDS activation
controls.barrier_role: Detect and respond to unintended EDS activation

supporting_text: Almost immediately the SDPO realized his mistake and he and the JDPO quickly started looking over the system to see if the EDS sequence initiated.
The moment the EDS function was initiated though, they realized that it did not make sense to test the EDS at this time and this must be a mistake.
The JDPO immediately called the Marco Polo production facility to notify them that they had just dropped the BOP stack

controls.name: Safe zone positioning for BOP operations
controls.barrier_role: Minimize impact of BOP drop on subsea infrastructure

supporting_text: The rig was located in the designated safe zone at the time of EDS activation and there was no subsea infrastructure impacted by the BOP being dropped.
The ROV then proceeded to the sea floor and found the BOPstack approximately 484 feet away from the well head, in the safe zone and away from all subsea infrastructure.

controls.name: ROV inspection and verification of BOP status
controls.barrier_role: Verify BOP disconnection and locate dropped equipment

supporting_text: The ROV dove to inspect the LMRP and BOP stack, and confirmed that the BOP stack was not connected to the LMRP.
The ROV then proceeded to the sea floor and found the BOPstack approximately 484 feet away from the well head, in the safe zone and away from all subsea infrastructure.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
